definition.name: Pataki_face_formula
definition.definition: (Pataki, 2000, Theorem 3.3.1). For a closed convex cone K in finite-dimensional real space, a linear map A : R^n → R^m, and the feasible set F = K ∩ {x : Ax = b}, the face dimension of a point x̄ ∈ F satisfies: dim(face(x̄, F)) = dim(face(x̄, K)) - m + dim(face(x̄, K)^⊥ ∩ R(A*)), where R(A*) is the range of the adjoint and ⊥ denotes the orthogonal complement within the linear span of face(x̄, K). For extreme points of F (LHS = 0): dim(face(x̄, K)) ≤ m. Applied to K = S^n_+ (real PSD cone) with face dimension r(r+1)/2, this gives the classical Barvinok-Pataki bound r(r+1)/2 ≤ m. The "correction term" dim(face(x̄, K)^⊥ ∩ R(A*)) measures degeneracy/implicit redundancy among constraints when restricted to the face. References: Pataki, Handbook of SDP (2000), Thm 3.3.1; Im-Wolkowicz, Oper. Res. Lett. 49(6), 2021.
